nodes.label: AI as tool for routine legal work
nodes.label_negative: AI hallucination and inaccuracy in legal research
nodes.grounding_span: I've noticed AIs are terrible looking them up and prone to hallucination

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: More collaboration, I haven't really worked with giving it a complete task

nodes.label: AI delegation for simple tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would be happy to delegate and focus my skills to the areas where they are needed

nodes.label: AI integration into workflow
nodes.grounding_span: Calendar integration, letting AI run my calendar. Though so far, I have been resistant as mistakes could be costly

nodes.label: Accuracy and compliance in legal work
nodes.grounding_span: I always double check. I never trust the AIs answer without my own verification

nodes.label: Professional efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I would be happy to delegate and focus my skills to the areas where they are needed

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I have been satisfied with basic memos, emails etc.

nodes.label: Frustration with research AI
nodes.grounding_span: I haven't really been frustrated except seeing that it is still quite limited with case research

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I would stay cautious but hope to expand

nodes.label: Caution toward AI in high-risk tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I have been resistant as mistakes could be costly

nodes.label: Verification-oriented — cross-checks AI outputs against other sources
nodes.grounding_span: I always double check. I never trust the AIs answer without my own verification

nodes.label: Pragmatic delegation with fallback confidence
nodes.grounding_span: If it becomes reliable in areas like docket management and case research, I would gladly let it handle those areas
